page: Vultr vhf-12c-48gb is an x86_64 architecture server within the High Frequency Compute family. It features 12 shared vCPUs mapped to 6 physical Intel cores with 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 4 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache, with hardware support for AVX, AES, BMI2, and FMA instructions. The system is equipped with 48.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, and includes a bundled 768 GB NVMe SSD for local storage alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance placing in the top 25% and single-threaded gzip compression reaching top-tier status in the top 10%. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, falls within the average range. Passmark memory testing ranks in the top 10%, though large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidth tests show weaker performance, placing in the bottom 25%. Database operations and Redis SET performance remain average, processing 13.71 million operations per second.

Because this server utilizes a shared CPU allocation, it presents a cost-efficient option for resource-dense workloads that require high single-threaded performance and fast local NVMe storage but do not demand sustained, dedicated multi-core execution. The weak large-block memory bandwidth and lower performance in medium-sized LLM inference tasks represent notable tradeoffs. This configuration is best suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, single-threaded data processing, and lightweight database operations.
description: Vultr vhf-12c-48gb is an x86_64 general-purpose server from the High Frequency Compute family, featuring 12 shared Intel vCPUs, 48.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 768 GB NVMe SSD. Operating at 2.0 GHz, the processor supports AVX, AES, and FMA instructions. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier single-threaded gzip compression, alongside high Passmark memory scores. However, multi-core operations and large-block uncached memory bandwidth are average to weak. The shared CPU allocation makes this instance a cost-effective choice for workloads requiring high single-threaded speed and fast local storage without the premium of dedicated cores. It is highly suitable for static web serving, database operations, and general-purpose applications.
og_description: Vultr vhf-12c-48gb is an x86_64 server with 12 shared Intel vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and a 768 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core performance and fast local storage for general-purpose and database workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vhf-12c-48gb features 12 shared Intel vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and a 768 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for general workloads.
tagline: A shared-CPU virtual server delivering strong single-threaded performance and fast NVMe storage for balanced general-purpose and database workloads.
bullet_points: 12 shared Intel vCPUs and 48.0 GB of system memory
Bundled 768 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Strong single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Cost-effective shared-resource model for non-dedicated workloads
Well-suited for static web serving and database operations
